name: Optimize CLI startup — eliminate dependency_injector from hot path

description: Baseline: save=290ms, search=308ms, list=318ms. Root cause: dependencies.py re-exports Container → forces 128ms dependency_injector load for every CLI command.

asi.bottleneck: dependencies.py re-exports Container from container.py at module level. Container's __getattr__ triggers lazy load of _container_di → dependency_injector (128ms). This happens for EVERY CLI command even though most don't need DI.
asi.fix_plan: Remove Container from dependencies.py re-exports. Only 0/29 CLI commands use get_container directly. Use lazy import in the few places that need it.

description: Lazy-load dependency_injector in CLI: remove dead re-exports from dependencies.py. save 290→186ms (36%), all commands 35-42% faster. 1678 tests pass.

asi.bottleneck: dependencies.py re-exported Container/create_container/get_container at module level, triggering dependency_injector (128ms) for every CLI command via __getattr__
asi.fix: Moved 12 unused re-exports to lazy __getattr__. Kept only 6 actually-used get_* functions as eager imports. DI no longer loaded for simple commands.

name: Optimize trifecta CLI startup for extension subprocess calls

description: Fast-path workspace detection: bypass DI container in detect_memory_db_path. DI never loaded for any CLI command. Updated tests.

asi.bottleneck: detect_memory_db_path called get_workspace_manager which went through get_container → dependency_injector (163ms). Replaced with _detect_workspace_fast that uses GitCommandExecutor directly (15ms).
asi.fix: New _detect_workspace_fast() bypasses DI. detect_memory_db_path calls it with exception handling. Updated tests to mock the new function.
asi.improvement: get_memory_service_auto: was triggering DI load, now DI=False. detect_memory_db_path: 170ms → 15ms (actual call). Total wall-clock similar because container.py import (56ms) unchanged.

description: Baseline: trifecta graph search = 111ms. 94% is CLI startup (Python imports). tree_sitter=502ms, regex=234ms imported but unused for graph search. cli.py imports 27 src modules at top level including ast_models (39ms) which pulls tree_sitter.

asi.bottleneck: cli.py imports 27 src modules at top level. ast_models pulls tree_sitter (502ms) even for graph search which doesn't need AST. Solution: lazy imports in cli.py for subcommand groups (ast, graph, ctx, etc.)

description: Lazy import ast_app + graph_app: first run 1091ms (JIT compilation), subsequent ~110ms. No improvement — lazy imports add overhead on first call and don't help subsequent calls since uv pre-compiles bytecode. The real bottleneck is Python interpreter startup (145ms), not module imports.

asi.hypothesis: Lazy import ast_app and graph_app in cli.py to avoid tree_sitter (502ms)
asi.result: No improvement. First run worse (1091ms JIT). Subsequent runs same as baseline (~110ms). uv tool install pre-compiles bytecode so lazy imports don't help.
asi.bottleneck: Python interpreter startup itself is 145ms — module imports are only 68ms on top of that. Even with zero imports, subprocess overhead + Python startup = ~80-100ms floor.
asi.real_solution: Need to eliminate subprocess entirely: persistent MCP server, or direct SQLite query from the extension
asi.next_action: Revert lazy imports, investigate MCP server or direct SQLite access
